chunks.title: Catalog Description
chunks.content: Computers and New Media. (3-0). Credit 3. This class investigates the potential and realized impact of computers in the design of new media, explores the variety of relationships between authors and readers of interactive materials, and explores the influence of media design and content expressed. Prerequisite: Graduate classification.

chunks.title: Course Learning Outcomes
chunks.content:
Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:
• analyze characteristics of computer-based media and anticipate likely reactions to them
• design software features that support immersion, agency, and transformation
• describe techniques for creating computer characters and narrative control
• develop software for the manipulation/use of audio and/or video
• describe core differences between physical and intellectual property
• assess characteristics of software licenses, software patents, and digital rights management software
• design, implement and evaluate software supporting the creation and/or consumption of content in specific settings

chunks.title: Grading Policy
chunks.content:
Homework: 10%
Team project: 35%
Readings write-ups: 35%
Survey paper: 10%
Attendance (via sign-in sheet): 10%
Grades will be on a 10 point scale: 90-100 = A, 80-89 = B, 70-79 = C, 60-69 = D, and <60 = F.

chunks.title: Late Work Policy
chunks.content: Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy (Student Rule 7).

chunks.title: Course Specific Late Work Policy
chunks.content:
Write-ups late policy:
50% is immediately deducted from your grade if turned in late. 5% more is deducted for every day late up to a maximum of one week after the original due date. For example, if a write up is turned in between 48-72 hours late, the highest grade it could receive is 40%. Do the reading and turn in the write-ups before class.
Other assignments late policy:
10% is deducted per day late up to a maximum of one week after the original due date.

chunks.title: Course Schedule
chunks.content:
week 1: introductions, history of media studies
week 2: designing software for immersion
week 3: designing software for agency and transformation
week 4: interactive media and computer characters
week 5: interactive text and social media
week 6: bridging physical and digital media
week 7: audio, music, and speech
week 8: recorded and authored video
week 9: games and computer-controlled characters
week 10: automatic narrative control
week 11: digital rights management
week 12: property rights and issues
week 13: software licenses and intellectual property
week 14: evaluating policy effects
week 15: student project presentations

chunks.title: Significant Due Dates
chunks.content:
Assignment 1 due January 26
Assignment 2 due February 16
Survey paper due May 1
Final project presentations due April 20-28
Final project reports due May 1
smaller project activities will be due once per week and readings write-ups will be due on average twice per week

chunks.title: Texas A&M College Station
chunks.content: Students needing a listening ear can contact University Health Services at 979.458.4584. Call 911 or visit your nearest emergency room if you are currently experiencing a life-threatening situation or if your safety is at risk. 24-hour emergency help is also available through the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline (988) or at 988lifeline.org.
